theScene: Curiously, you step towards the building labeled 'Arcade', its many doors kept constantly open as people move in and out of the premises, chatting and laughing about this and that under the glaringly bright glow of blinking neon lights. As you walk by, you notice odd turnstyle devices rowed up next to the entrance, a variety of Delusian denizens slotting Eros into the machines and seemingly getting some sort of capsules in exchange.

Shelving that consideration for later, you step into the building itself, immediately trading the noisy hustle and bustle of the Eros District's streets for the {i}even noisier{/i} cacophony of sounds coming from both excited locals and a flaring myriad of devices that shine with colorful imagery of all kinds in the intentionally darkly lit interior, the contraptions clearly appearing to be gaming machines of various sorts.

You can't help but feel a little... lost in this strange environment, much as you were upon your arrival in Ars Delusia, so many strange unfamiliar sounds and sights blaring and flashing across the large spacious room stuffed with people and games alike...

Disoriented as you are, you belatedly notice the eerily ghostly outline of some other adventurer and a monster advancing upon you, making you tense up with confusion as they walk into you... only for them to pass right through you without slowing their stride, as though you're not even there?!

Blinking blearily at the already receding shapes of the clearly not ghosts, you find yourself at a loss of what to make of this, but before you can ponder the confounding occurrence, a strikingly familiar feline-fashioned woman steps up to you and lightly nudges your side with a sleeve-covered gooey hand, snapping you out of your half-stupefied daze with a snicker.
Heya, first-timer. Before you get totally lost, welcome to the Arcade.

She states rather plainly and cuttingly, her almost harsh tone at least having the merit of grounding you back to reality, focusing your attention on her while she considers you with a laid-back snarky expression.
... Then, as you look to her and her to you, her eyes narrow on you with a brief pause of realization, her voice getting even sharper when she again speaks up.
... Wait, you're the guy from that whole fiasco with that red slime...
Though I've been told you both got out of that whole thing pretty much fine, so that's good... Even if that also meant I had to get grilled for hours by Voltlin security... {i}Again...{/i}
Why does weird shit have to keep happening in front of my Arcade, goddess damn it...
'If you're {i}that{/i} annoyed about it, I can just leave.'

Urgh... Just the usual big-city crap: sex bouts breaking out, under-the-table deals, lurking gangs... or simply people getting heated, causing a scene, and then getting arrested for public disturbance.

Since this is a place where a lot of people come and go, there isn't a lot of surveillance, and customers are left to their own devices, that makes it convenient for Livewires, petty criminals, and the like to do their thing on the down-low.

But as I've told the Voltlins and let them check like a {i}hundred times{/i}: no, my Arcade {i}isn't{/i} a hub of backdoor deals, trafficking, or any other illegal shit! {i}This. Is. A. Legitimate. Business{/i}!

Concluding her ranting outburst with an angry huff, she decisively slams her foot down on the ground with a gooey splat before carrying on in a somewhat calmer voice, going back to a more restrained mask-muffled mutter.
{size=-3}All I want is for people to have their fun and leave each other and {b}me{/b} in peace, dammit...{/size}

She looks about to automatically snap back, but stops herself in mild surprise at your unexpectedly meek response, eyeing you perplexedly for a moment before looking away with an irked gooey click of her slimy tongue.
Hmph, you don't need to apologize for that, dude. It's not like you had anything to do with it...
{i}I know, I know...{/i} And I'm not blaming you, dude... I know you just got caught up in that whole craziness and did what you could.
... And for the record, I think it's genuinely pretty impressive that you were able to deal with it all on your own.
For a moment, her expression softens as she nods in approval, but it's not long before her face clouds up in annoyance again and she sighs ruefully.
I'm just wishing the bullshit would stop for, like, a week... or even just a day or two.

EventText.NameOfScene: 'If you're {i}that{/i} annoyed about it, I can just leave.'
theScene: There is a ticked-off glare in her eyes as she takes in your testy response, but after a tense pause, she just coolly shrugs.

She then turns away from you and steps over to a spacious and colorful counter littered with what look to be prizes and tickets, lifting the partition to settle behind, squishily easing herself into a swivel chair, leaving you to your own devices in the flashing wonderland of colors and beeps...

Leaving it at that, she moves on with a miffed huff and a shake of her head, the tail on her hoodie seeming to somehow accompany her movements by tensing and swaying on its own as she reflexively tugs down her face mask and pulls a sucker out of seemingly nowhere, starting to lazily lick the sugary treat while she carries on.

{i}Anyways.{/i} Back to when you first came in, you must've been confused by that 'ghostly' pair that passed through you, right? That's 'cause the magic over Lucidia does some funky junk when there's lots of people in one place, so that more people than normally possible can be in a same building or wherever... I guess the idea is for people to mingle without bothering each other? It's weird. Might've happened to you on the street, too...

{i}Anyways.{/i} Back to the present, you must've been confused by that 'ghostly' pair that passed through you a bit ago, huh? That's 'cause the magic over Lucidia does some funky junk when there's lots of people in one place, so that more people than normally possible can be in a same building or wherever... I guess the idea is for people to mingle without bothering each other? It's weird. Might've happened to you on the street, too...

Buuuut that aside, once more: Welcome to the Arcade! If you wanna play something, just go put Eros into one of the cabinets and go nuts. Depending on your score, you'll get tickets, which you can redeem with me at the counter for prizes.

Somewhat half-heartedly, she gives you a thumbs up with her free hand to conclude her explanation, the cheerful gesture clashing rather starkly with her naturally frowny expression... which gets even grumpier as something else strikes her, her mouth clenching into an angry wince.
Ah. Just- {i}Don't{/i} smash the machines. I'm serious. Nothing's gonna jump out of them and try to kill you or whatever-

I've had to fix way too many of the cabinets because of newbies freaking out... Voltlin tech doesn't come cheap, y'know? I swear, seeing how some people react, you'd think they've been living under a rock for the last hundred years...
So yeah: {i}please{/i} don't hit them. Apart from that, feel free to check out the place and knock yourself out.

She finishes with a pat on your shoulder, then goes back to licking her sucker idly, stepping over to a spacious and colorful counter littered with what look to be prizes and tickets, lifting the partition to settle behind, squishily easing herself into a swivel chair before addressing you a final nod.
If you've got any questions, shoot, but otherwise, just go and have fun, dude.
She turns away as her attention is soon requested by another Arcade-goer who walks over tickets in hand, leaving you to your own devices in the flashing wonderland of colors and beeps...

You step towards the building labeled 'Arcade', its many doors kept constantly open as people move in and out of the premises, chatting and laughing about this and that under the glaringly bright glow of blinking neon lights. As you walk by, you notice odd turnstyle devices rowed up next to the entrance, a variety of Delusian denizens slotting Eros into the machines and seemingly getting some sort of capsules in exchange.

Stepping into the building itself, you immediately trade the noisy hustle and bustle of the Eros District's streets for the {i}even noisier{/i} cacophony of sounds coming from both excited locals and a flaring myriad of devices that shine with colorful imagery of all kinds in the intentionally darkly lit interior, making for an almost otherworldly atmosphere.

After taking a moment to readjust yourself, you scan your surroundings, finding them pretty much the same as last time: in every direction, cabinets of various shapes and sizes beckon and entice with all sorts of games to play, while front and center, the 'boss' of the Arcade sits behind her reception desk, idly watching over her clientele.
As you go to use the Arcade machine, you realize you don't actually have enough money to play...

You step out of the cloistered and light flaring interior of the Arcade back out into the hustle and bustle of the busy Eros district streets, blinking blearily at the change in lighting and atmosphere.

While most of the Arcade-goers seem to have come in pairs or groups, one uniformed Voltlin stands out, being on her own and positively glued to one of the cabinets, an expression of intense concentration on her face as her eyes follow the moving pixelated shapes and her hands wield the control pad with practiced ease. Judging by the characteristic bags under her eyes and the frown on her forehead, you're pretty sure this is Lovol.

While most of the Arcade-goers seem to have come in pairs or groups, one uniformed Voltlin stands out, being on her own and positively glued to one of the cabinets, an expression of intense concentration on her face as her eyes follow the moving pixelated shapes and her hands wield the control pad with practiced ease.
You focus back on the machines in front of you, leaving alone the diminutive monster girl who does not even seem to have noticed you.

You step towards the seemingly mesmerized Voltlin, but to your surprise, when you open your mouth to speak, she interrupts you by talking first, all while continuing to play, her eyes still fixed on the colorful screen while her fingers remain a blur of movement.
Sorry, I'm busy and I didn't come here to make friends anyway. Have a nice day, or whatever.

She drones in the same breath, rebuffing you rather peremptorily and leaving you little choice but to focus your attention on something else... You guess that if you came to know her in different circumstances, she might become more approachable?
As you step towards Lovol, her surprisingly alert eyes suddenly dart to you, and an equally surprising smile forms on her face.

The sound of Lovol's voice drawing your attention back to her, you flusteredly answer with an apologetic mumble as you focus back on the task at hand, the Voltlin reacting to your hesitation with a smirk.
Don't worry about everyone else, they can't really see you... Though maybe that's a disappointment for you?

She gives you a quick goodbye nod then immediately returns her attention to the game, letting you carry on with your day.
See what {i}Slime Queen's Parade{/i} is about.

EventText.NameOfScene: See what {i}Slime Queen's Parade{/i} is about.
theScene: {i}Slime Queen's Parade{/i} features the journey of an enterprising Slime Queen, recognisable by her regal crown, on her way to founding a new slime kingdom. Toward this noble purpose, she roams her lands' verdant, fertile plains in search of fruit to attract new gooey subjects, thus forming her slimedom's thriving populace!.

To determine when to end her endeavors, the Slime Queen has a simple method: whenever she bumps into another slime, she considers that any further population increase would be too much, and stops there, thus finishing the game. The Slime Queen is also very ditzy, and prone to getting immediately and hopelessly lost should she venture out of familiar territory, so she must be careful not to stray too far!

Your goal is thus to keep the slime train going unperturbed for as long as possible, all the while making sure not to go out of bounds, in order to earn a maximum of points! 500 points gives 1 ticket, and the number of points granted by each type of fruit is as follows:

Blueberry - 100 points
Cherry - 250 points
Appel - 500 points ({i}Not a typo, stop telling me to fix it - Noir{/i})
Marshmallow - 1000 points ({i}Not a fruit, I know. Don't mind it.{/i})
Peach - 2500 points (Only appears after reaching 5000 points.)
Grape - 5000 points (Only appears after reaching 10000 points.)

When you insert your token into the cabinet's slot with an emphatic mechanical {i}ka-chunk{/i}, cheerful music rings out while colorful displays flash and blink to accompany your coin's journey down the machine...

Your token drops with a {i}ping{/i} as it hits a prong, bouncing off to the left and thunking down along a track of perilous obstacles, more lights flaring as it rattles, careens, and flies across the field...

... then hits a large circular bumper which immediately activates and sends your token on a merry bounce further left, clacking against one last prong as it teeters between the 10 and 25-ticket slots-!
... then bounces off a particularly large circular bumper, pinging onward on its final plunge towards the 10 and 1-ticket slots-!

Your token drops down with a {i}thonk{/i} as it lands on a prong and clatters its way to the right with a few bounces, more lights accenting its descent as it falls off the stairway towards a bumper...

... which it hits squarely, energetically bouncing back into the air, clanking off another prong and spinning to the left, rattling as it lands on a coiled track to roll its way towards either falling off and landing into the 10-ticket slot, or successfully going all the way and reaching the 50-ticket slot!

... which it strikes at an unfortunate angle, pinging off the bumper and flying to the right, clacking against the wall before falling down towards another prong that will send it careening down either into a 1-ticket slot or a 10-ticket slot!

Your token manages to bounce off a prong and ricochet to the top of the machine before smoothly diving down the center path of the machine where few obstacles lay, the acrobatic maneuver letting it vertiginously plunge towards either a 50-ticket slot or a 1-ticket slot-!

... And with a wild flare of congratulatory lights and music, the token lands unerringly straight into the 50 slot, the machine whirring out your hefty amount of tickets while the triumphant jingle bombastically plays and a colorful show of cascading lights celebrates your success!
... And with a cheerful fanfare, your token lands dead center into the 25 slot, the machine whirring out your tickets as the victory jingle plays!
... And with a short fanfare of sounds and lights, your token lands in the 10 slot, the machine whirring out your tickets as the brief jingle plays!
... And with a singular, sad beep, your token lands in the 1 slot, the machine whirring out your single Arcade ticket while a sorrowful (TwT) shows on the display screen...

Looking over the machine, it looks like you just insert an Arcade token into an upper slot to let the coin fall down, bouncing off prongs and other obstacles to ultimately land in a marked slot at the bottom. Said slots are marked '50', '25', '10', and '1', your result netting you the corresponding amount of tickets... along with a colorful show of sounds and lights.

You wonder if you're missing something and whether the cabinet has some subtle, hidden controls that elude you, but it does seem like that's all there is to it: you don't actually have any input on your token's fate, which is entirely left to the whims of chance. It's all based on {i}Luck{/i}.

Approaching the glass case counter of the Arcade, you can see a myriad of colorful prizes lined up on top of it and within its confines, its interior clearly holding the more valuable ticket prizes while the wall behind the purple slime proprietress primarily sports plush toys and assorted trinkets and doodads, the gooey owner giving you an impassive stare while her feline hoodie tail flicks curiously at your coming.
The purple slime raises an appraising eyebrow as you approach her, watching you curiously while a mischievous smile forms beneath her mask.
Hm? I thought you wanted to leave. Changed your mind?

Somehow, she looks almost cheerful, and doesn't seem to expect you to elaborate on your change of heart, though she quickly catches herself and chases the apparently unbidden smile from her face, her expression reverting to a more customary frowny one with a mild huff.
Your upfront apology seems to take her by surprise, the slime girl looking perplexed for a moment before she looks away and wetly clicks her tongue.
It's... fine, okay? No need to bring out the violins...
You feel the slime girl's gaze on your back as you turn around and walk away...
... then the feeling subsides as the gooey proprietress directs her attention towards something else, leaving you back to your own devices.
... Oh, and before I forget: name's Noir. Just in case you'd want to know.
No need to tell me {i}your{/i} name, though: that cherry girl wouldn't stop saying it back then, so I remember it. [ThePlayerName], right?
You confirm, prompting Noir to give a brief content little chuckle.
But anyways, don't let me keep you: go and enjoy the Arcade, and if you've got any questions... Well, I'm here.
As you approach the glass case counter of the Arcade, the purple slime proprietress settled behind it gives you a small half-hearted wave.
Before you can answer, however, she seems to realize something and snaps her fingers with a wet noise.

Ah, wait, I just remembered I totally spaced introductions when you got here... You know, what with trying to help you adjust to all the noise and new stuff... Sooo... name's Noir. I hope you're enjoying the Arcade.
Oh, and no need to tell me {i}your{/i} name: that cherry girl wouldn't stop saying it back then, so I remember it. [ThePlayerName], right?
You confirm, and Noir gives a brief content little chuckle before carrying on.
I knew it... But anyways, what's up? Here to exchange tickets?

With a sigh, Noir leans forward onto the counter's glass case, the little cat bell on her hoodie jingling slightly as she tugs down her face mask while drawing out a lollipop to lazily lick, giving you an almost dismissive side glance.
Sure, I guess... So... Questions about the Arcade? Or something else?
'What kind of machine are the Arcade cabinets? How do they work?'
'I've never seen a slime with feet before? ...Or a cat's tail.'
'I don't normally see slimes with clothing...'

Noir sucks the heart-shaped candy off its stick, letting it dissolve away inside her gooey form while she pulls her mask back up with a sigh, seeming slightly miffed at seeing her impromptu break come to an end.
Gotcha... Did you need anything else? Any prize exchanging?

EventText.NameOfScene: 'What kind of machine are the Arcade cabinets? How do they work?'
theScene: Well, that's just- probably {i}way{/i} more of a complex question than you realize...
She sucks on her lollipop thoughtfully for a moment as she ponders how to present you with an answer, eventually carrying on.
The short version is that it's Voltlin tech based on stuff they unearthed in the city... Back before they fixed up the place and made it livable and people started moving in.

To be honest, even if those babies are my bread and butter, even I don't really know how the tech works exactly... but I {i}do{/i} know how to work with it. And how to deal with issues like, y'know, popping one of those suckers open and fixing a piece that's on the fritz... Though really, resetting the machine is enough to put it back in working order most of the time. That stuff's pretty resilient.

And good thing too, because boy do the Volts' charge out the ass for repairs. That's the perks of exclusivity for you... But at least, they won't scam me, because they know that unlike {i}too many{/i} Delusians, I know my stuff and I can be trusted with that kind of hardware.
After taking a quick lick of her candy, she puffs up with a bit of smug pride, the feline tail attached to her hoodie swaying behind her with lazy enthusiasm.
And on that note, FYI, I made the first Slime Queen's Parade cabinet myself. Y'can see my name in the Credits and all.♪

It's actually also how I got to be in charge of this place. Used to be run by Voltlins, but 'cause they kept having weird political drama over how to manage the Arcade, they eventually decided it'd be better to let a third party handle it, and they held a game-making contest for the position.
Ehehe~ If I'm honest, I didn't make the {i}best{/i} game, but I fit all the criteria perfectly.♪

She seems lost in her own world of fond reminiscence for a few moments before returning her attention to you, shifting back into her usual nonchalant behavior, her sucker plopped into her mouth while she waits for your next question.

She blinks in mild surprise, needing a moment to process your question before her eyes light up in understanding and she uses the arm she seemingly always keeps in her pocket to point at the purple and black device resting on her shoulders and neck.
Uuh, this-? That what you mean? ...Man, I basically forgot it was even there-
She pulls the item off and presents it to you invitingly with an unusually cheerful grin on her face.

It's a headset! It's essentially a pair of speakers... er, 'sound emitters'? hooked up to a device that plays music. Bring your ear closer and take a listen. Normally, you'd just put 'em both over your ears to enjoy the tunes, but I can't do that while I'm working or I wouldn't hear jack all.

Curiously, you lean in towards the strange device and can indeed hear surprisingly high-energy music pulsing from it as though it were being played right inside the contraption, though Noir shortly pulls the headset away to set it back onto her shoulders, leaving you a bit dazed by the intense beats that somehow sounded even stranger and more chaotic than the high-tech cacophony drifting through the Arcade.
Noir, for her part, is still all smiles, either not realizing your confused reaction or not minding it, the hooded slime instead chuckling proudly.

Cool, right? I got them specially made to go with my whole aesthetic. You can actually purchase a pair pretty much just like this one with tickets, so if you enjoy the idea of music on the go, here's an added incentive for ya.♪ It's top of the line stuff, too: Voltlin-made, with cutting-edge circuitry, high-quality baffles, durable padding... The whole nine yards!
And because I'm a slime, I can {i}really{/i} feel the music's vibrations going through me. It feels really-
She goes on with an increasingly dreamy and naughty expression before abruptly cutting herself off with a cough and going back to licking at her sucker while mumbling.
Uh... Hrm. Anyways, d'you have any other questions?

EventText.NameOfScene: 'I've never seen a slime with feet before? ...Or a cat's tail.'
theScene: Hrm? As weird as that {i}sounds{/i} out loud, it's not exactly surprising...
To get that out of the way, though: I don't actually have a cat tail or ears, though I can kinda shape my slime to mimic them within the hoodie.

You can't help but discern a shade of bitter peevishness in her voice as she speaks while the feline tail attached to her cat-themed hoodie flicks idly behind her and she licks at her hard candy with a vengeance, until she shelves that thought and swaps her frustrated expression for a smug grin.

As for the legs and feet... that's a purple slime special.♪ We're kind of like the opposite of green slimes, with very solid and firm goo. Lots of... what's it called... Liquid tension? Y'know, more... {i}togetherness{/i}. Less mess, at the very least.
She goes back to thoughtfully lapping at her sucker for a bit, shrugging as she carries on conversationally.
Well, I say purple slime, but dark slime is probably more correct, I guess? Slime classifications can get weird, and depending on who you ask, it's arguably just semantics...

'Cause I mean, dark slimes like me can happen from a few things, that being our favorite foods: grapes, candy, blackberry... and that aphrodisiac smog that permeates the city. Just walking around town feels like eating. Convenient.♪
She smacks her lips rather seductively over her confection to punctuate the word, then segues into a lazy shrug as she wraps up her explanation.
Though don't ask me to get further than that and go into a whole encyclopedic presentation. It's not like I'm some kind of slime expert or something.

EventText.NameOfScene: 'I don't normally see slimes with clothing...'
theScene: Prrrrobably because {i}they'd{/i} get it all goopy and wet... and then what's the point? It would be all icky to wear, and they'd look like such a mess that it would defeat the whole idea of wearing clothes for fashion.

'Sides, most slimes don't really care about that stuff anyways, hence why you'll most often see them in the buff. Not much of a point in looking for clothes when you have no interest in wearing anything but what nature gave ya.

I do, though. A {i}lot{/i}... I guess you could call that 'fashion-conscious'? Especially about the hoodie. This thing isn't cheap, either, and I got five copies upstairs in my room... Used to be six, but a {i}certain explosion{/i} killed that one.

She lets out a heavy huffy sigh as she continues talking, shifting in place as though cozily cuddling into the clothing she's wearing while waving the sucker in her hand at you in a dismissive gesture, all the while her hoodie's tail reflexively flicks to the side.
So yeah, essentially, it's comfy, cozy, makes me not naked, and fits my aesthetic.
She then plops the candy back into her mouth to conclude her answer... But since you're on the subject, you take that opportunity to also inquire about the strange face mask she's wearing.

Hrm? Oh, the Livewires... Er, they're a Voltlin... {i}artistic organization{/i} of sorts. They started up that fashion trend and, well, I kinda dug it... It's not for health reasons or anything, I just think it looks cool.

Also, it... uh... stops me from unintentionally scowling at people as much... Not that I have anything against anyone in particular. Just an RBF kind of thing... But you know some people can take that personally.
She disaffectedly shrugs at that with an 'Oh well.' expression, then goes back to leisurely licking at her lollipop with a questioning look your way.
Anyways, you got any more questions?
Getting a little {i}personal{/i} with that one.
She narrows her eyes at you inquisitively, looking you up and down as if considering whether that qualifies as a 'weird question', before ultimately relenting with a shrug.

But I s'pose most of the slimes you've met are probably from outside of cities... And I know most of 'em are perfectly fine living in the Forest or thereabouts, so I guess I can understand how a 'high-tech' slime comes off as something of an outlier.
But yeah, I'm from Ars Delusia, born and raised in the big city... Though I guess 'born' doesn't fully fit how those things work for slimes, but let's not get into that.

Suffice to say I'm a city slime through and through. I honestly can't imagine living anywhere else. No offense to most slimes, but I feel like the average slime's lifestyle would make me go crazy {i}fast{/i}. The Capital is where it's at!

...On that note, a lot of you adventurer types come to sing a similar tune eventually, though I'm pretty sure that's mostly due to the horny succubi, high-energy Voltlins, and all the other sex-hungry monsters, rather than because of all the cool techy stuff... But hey, I could be wrong.

Honestly, though, there's a {i}lot{/i} to like about this city: all kinds of cuisines, fashions, and entertainments you can't find anywhere else, the hustle and bustle of the streets with all the people...
She actually gets a dreamy look in her eyes for a moment, which she shakes off with a good-humored snicker.

Hah, not like I actually get out of the Arcade much, though. But maybe I'm a weirdo either way... Which works for me: being a weird girl managing a weird place for weird people in a weird city... that suits me fine!
She concludes with one last chuckle before returning to her usual laid-back attitude and going back to idly sucking at her lollipop.
Anyways, I hope that satiated your curiosity. Got any other questions?

Noir lazily gestures over to the prizes both on the wall behind her and in the glass case in front of you, as well as within the glass fridge behind the counter. You have [ProgressDisplay] Arcade tickets.
Noir flatly stares at you as she points out you dont have enough tickets to get that.

These sweet and sour gummi slimes look quite familiar, apparently coming from a 'special artisan candy shop'. Consuming them restores 50 Energy and removes paralysis. You have [ProgressDisplay] Arcade tickets to spend.

These sweet and sour gummi slimes look like they come from a special artisan candy shop of sorts. Consuming them restores 50 Energy and removes paralysis. You have [ProgressDisplay] Arcade tickets to spend.
Get a Bag of Gummi Slimes. (5 Tickets)
Get 5 Bags of Gummi Slimes. (25 Tickets)
Get 10 Bags of Gummi Slimes. (50 Tickets)

EventText.NameOfScene: Get a Bag of Gummi Slimes. (5 Tickets)
Get 5 Bags of Gummi Slimes. (25 Tickets)
Get 10 Bags of Gummi Slimes. (50 Tickets)

theScene: Looking at the colorfully adorned can, the strange beverage is likely to fill you with 50% of you energy while dealing 50 arousal to you from the sheer energizing effects of the tasty liquid. You have [ProgressDisplay] Arcade tickets to spend.
Get Can of Static Rush. (10 Tickets)
Get 5 Cans of Static Rush. (50 Tickets)
Get 10 Cans of Static Rush. (100 Tickets)

EventText.NameOfScene: Get Can of Static Rush. (10 Tickets)
Get 5 Cans of Static Rush. (50 Tickets)
Get 10 Cans of Static Rush. (100 Tickets)

theScene: Among the available prizes sits a singular piece of draconically styled chewing gum that allegedly stimulates the mind, opening the recipient to new avenues of self-improvement, thus granting 250 EXP when consumed. You have [ProgressDisplay] Arcade tickets to spend.
Get Dragon Bubble Puzzle Gum. (25 Tickets)
Get 5 Dragon Bubble Puzzle Gum. (125 Tickets)
Get 10 Dragon Bubble Puzzle Gum. (250 Tickets)

EventText.NameOfScene: Get Dragon Bubble Puzzle Gum. (25 Tickets)
Get 5 Dragon Bubble Puzzle Gum. (125 Tickets)
Get 10 Dragon Bubble Puzzle Gum. (250 Tickets)

theScene: This small, darkly colored can has streaks of sharp white splashed across it like rays of piercing light. Consuming it restores 100% of your Energy while removing the Sleep status effect. You have [ProgressDisplay] Arcade tickets to spend.
Get Can of Night Bane. (50 Tickets)
Get 5 Cans of Night Bane. (250 Tickets)
Get 10 Cans of Night Bane. (500 Tickets)

EventText.NameOfScene: Get Can of Night Bane. (50 Tickets)
Get 5 Cans of Night Bane. (250 Tickets)
Get 10 Cans of Night Bane. (500 Tickets)

theScene: The purple and black headphones sitting in the display case look marked to increase the wearers resistance to Charm and Trance by 50%, while lowering Sleep resistance by 50%. You have [ProgressDisplay] Arcade tickets to spend.

There's a cat bell on a cushion inside the glass case that exudes a strange energy from it... It apparenly increases your luck by 9, and your arousal dealt from fucking by 15%. You have [ProgressDisplay] Arcade tickets to spend.
Get the Eros District Stamp. (250 Tickets)
Is that a real Sigil Hunt stamp?
The purple slime, who had been idly watching over the Arcade while you were perusing the prizes, raises an eyebrow at your question and grumbles.
... Didn't I explain that earlier? You know, earn tickets and turn them in for prizes? The same goes for this stamp as for anything else here.

But don't worry, I'm sure you'll have fun gathering tickets for it. And if my humble little den of distractions is somehow {i}too{/i} challenging for a big seasoned adventurer like you, there's always other stamps in other districts. So no pressure.

EventText.NameOfScene: Is that a real Sigil Hunt stamp?
theScene: Hmph... Yes, it {i}is{/i} real and official. No gotcha, no catch, no hidden clauses, no strings attached, and no fakes. I'm getting enough regular visits from some ADS agent coming in and checking it from every angle like it's a relic from the first Demon Queen to be sure of that.

Diverting her gaze from you, she mumbles something indistinct while deftly pulling down her mask, unwrapping a lollipop, and attacking it with ferocious licks, though she abruptly stops after a few seconds, something else seeming to have struck her as she returns her attention to you with a grimace.
... Then again, considering the kind of tricks some people can pull off, I s'pose I can't blame you for worrying about it being some kind of scam...

But yeah, no worries on that front: only place to get the Eros District stamp is right here. I even have a certificate and everything, though I'd rather you don't ask me to get it, since I put it somewhere out back and it'd be a hassle to find it.

... And before you go asking why it's just sitting there in the middle of the other prizes instead of me advertizing it, the answer is that I'm not supposed to. That's meant to be part of the challenge of finding the stamps, y'know.

Emphasizing that additional piece of information with a shrug, she pulls the now bare stick of her rapidly dissolved sucker out of her mouth and tosses it into a nearby can with practised ease, before silently putting her mask back up and leisurely leaning back as she awaits your next move.

EventText.NameOfScene: Get the Eros District Stamp. (250 Tickets)
theScene: You hand your tickets over to Noir who lets out a huff as she wordlessly pulls down her mask and pops a fresh sucker into her mouth before briskly receiving, counting, and stashing your tickets behind the counter, then crouching to slide open the glass case door of your prize which she plucks with two gooey fingers before rising back to gaze at you expectantly.

While you get out your card, the slime woman idly twirls the stamper between her fingers and lazily enjoys her treat, but the second you put down the little paper on the counter, her hand darts down in a blink-and-you-miss-it moment, the stamper making a satisfying click on impact and leaving behind it a neat purple ink stamp in the shape of an ero coin!

While you're retrieving your well-earned stamped card and enjoying a good look at it, Noir has already nimbly stashed the stamper back within its glass case next to its little sign, and proceeds to carefully close the little sliding door while keeping the sucker in her mouth before popping back up with a springy bounce and a rather cheerful grin.
Well, congrats. I hope you've enjoyed your time at the Arcade so far and will swing by again.

She pauses for a moment with a relaxed look while the rest of her candy dissolves within her jellowy confines, but then suddenly lets out a 'Oh, right!' and leans to the side, opening a drawer to take out what looks like a sheet of parchment which she sets down before clearing her throat... Or is it her goo? She at least makes the motions and sounds you'd expect before exclaiming in a surprisingly animated and theatrical manner.

{i}Congratulations, oh brave adventurer! You have bested my den of devious digital distractions by dint of discernment, serendipity, and diligence! ... Yet, still more trials doubtless await you across your future adventures, so go forth with your wits sharpened to a point and your victory certain!{/i}

Without skipping a beat, she then takes out a small colorful cone from behind the counter and pulls a string dangling from it, whereupon the papery contraption opens with a a small pop, resoundingly shooting confetti into the air which rain upon your somewhat bemused person while Noir slimily claps, before eventually letting out a little chuckle.
... Okay, {i}now{/i} I'm done. Anything else you want to exchange tickets for?

She nonchalantly asks while breezily re-masking herself and tossing away the spent cone, then leaning on the counter with a proud cat-like smile on her lips while her hoodie's tail sways behind her contentedly, the manager seeming quite happy with herself and looking at you with some lingering amusement as you brush away the dusting of confetti on your hair and clothes before stashing away your freshly stamped card.

On the shelf under the prize wall behind Noir sits a clearly hand-sewn black cat plushie sporting deep purple accents. A small tag next to it informs you that it increases max Energy by 250 and increases status effect duration and application chance by 15%, but that its ill-omen nature lowers Luck by 9. You have [ProgressDisplay] Arcade tickets to spend.
